title: Forest-Fire Watchtowers: Maximum Interference-Free Channel Completion (Gerechte Design)
strategies: tune-nothing baseline: echo only the pre-tuned givens (reproduces the checker baseline, ~0.1)
row-major smallest-channel greedy: fast but paints itself into corners, leaving many towers untuned
most-constrained-variable (MRV) greedy ordering: tunes the tightest sites first, reaches more towers
MRV backtracking search for a full gerechte (row + column + sector) completion, with a deterministic node budget and best-partial recording -- tunes the most towers
